title: STANDARD FOR DURUM WHEAT SEMOLINA AND DURUM WHEAT FLOUR

sections.section: STANDARD FOR DURUM WHEAT SEMOLINA AND DURUM WHEAT FLOUR
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Durum Wheat Semolina and Durum Wheat Flour.
Document code: CXS 178-1991.

sections.section: Adopted in 1991. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2019, 2023.
sections.summary: Adoption history noting the standard was adopted in 1991, revised in 1995, and amended in 2019 and 2023.
Amendment note stating that, following decisions at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, amendments were made in the Annex.

Scope: the standard applies to durum wheat semolina (including whole semolina) and durum wheat flour prepared from Triticum durum Desf. for direct human consumption, prepackaged for retail or destined for use in other food products, and does not apply to products from common or club wheat or their mixtures with durum, nor to semolina/flour intended for non-food industrial or animal feed use.
Heading only: description of the commodity (details follow in the text).

Quality obligations: durum wheat semolina and flour (and any added nutrients) must be safe and fit for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours, odours and living insects, and free from filth (animal-origin impurities, including dead insects) in amounts that may pose a health hazard.
Moisture limit: maximum moisture content 14.5% m/m; lower moisture limits may be required for certain destinations based on climate, and duration of transport and storage.
States the moisture content limit: 14.5 percent m/m maximum.

Contaminants requirements: durum wheat semolina and flour must be free from heavy metals at levels that may hazard health, and must comply with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticide residues and with Codex maximum limits for mycotoxins for this commodity.
Durum wheat semolina and durum wheat flour shall be free from heavy metals at levels that may represent a hazard to health.
Durum wheat semolina and durum wheat flour must comply with maximum pesticide residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission for this commodity.
Durum wheat semolina and durum wheat flour must comply with maximum mycotoxin limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission for this commodity.

Hygiene requirements recommend following the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene and, as practicable under good manufacturing practice, the product must be free from objectionable matter and from microorganisms, parasites, or microbial-origin substances in amounts that may represent a health hazard (when tested by appropriate methods).

sections.section: 5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969), 1  and other codes of practice recommended by the CAC which are relevant to this product.
sections.summary: It is recommended that the product be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant CAC codes of practice.

sections.section: 5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
sections.summary: To the extent possible under good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.

sections.section: 5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product
sections.summary: When tested by appropriate sampling and examination methods, the product shall be free from microorganisms and parasites, and shall not contain substances originating from microorganisms, in amounts that may represent a hazard to health.

Packaging must safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities of durum wheat semolina and flour; containers and materials must be safe and not impart toxic substances or undesirable odour/flavour, and sacks must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

sections.section: 6.1 Durum wheat semolina and durum wheat flour shall  be  packaged  in  containers  which  will  safeguard  the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product.
sections.summary: Durum wheat semolina and durum wheat flour shall be packaged in containers that safeguard their hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities.

sections.section: 6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their  intended  use.  They  should  not  impart  any  toxic  substance  or  undesirable  odour  or  flavour  to  the product.
sections.summary: Containers and packaging materials shall be made of substances safe and suitable for their intended use and shall not impart toxic substances or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.

sections.section: 6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.
sections.summary: When packaged in sacks, the sacks must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.
Heading: labelling must comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) with additional product-specific provisions (see following clauses).
The product name shown on the label shall be 'durum wheat semolina', 'whole durum wheat semolina', or 'durum wheat flour' as appropriate.

Information for non-retail containers may appear on the container or in accompanying documents, but the product name, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer/packer must appear on the container, and lot identification and manufacturer/packer details may be replaced by an identifiable identification mark linked to accompanying documents.
Methods of analysis and sampling specified in Codex Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard shall be used to check compliance.

Users are strongly recommended to specify the appropriate factor limits and methods of analysis where more than one is given; specified limits include ash (max: semolina 1.3% db, whole semolina 2.1% db, flour 1.75% db), protein (N×5.7 min: semolina 10.5% db, whole semolina 11.5% db, flour 11.0% db), nutrients as per national legislation, and particle size (semolina max 79% passing 315 μm; flour min 80% passing 315 μm).
